summary: The extraction-fidelity lane accepts the candidate because the proposed result update uses the retained full-text adverse-event table Overall row, preserves 5/30 control and 23/30 D+Q arm counts, calculates the risk ratio and approximate log-scale uncertainty from those counts, and leaves preferred-term rows with blank control cells descriptive rather than converting them to zero.

findings.claim_or_issue: The result records Overall adverse-event counts of 5/30 untreated control and 23/30 D+Q participants from Extended Data Table 1.
findings.why_it_matters: Those are the only adverse-event term-table counts in this candidate that support a comparative overall-row effect.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the Overall-row event_specific_counts and source-snapshot/table provenance as written.

findings.claim_or_issue: The candidate calculates RR 4.60 from (23/30)/(5/30), with log risk-ratio standard error 0.42049 and approximate 95% CI 2.02 to 10.49.
findings.why_it_matters: The effect.value and uncertainty fields are derived directly from reported arm counts rather than inferred from blank preferred-term cells.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the calculation note and keep the effect scoped to the Overall row only.

findings.claim_or_issue: The result caveats and adverse_event.zero_handling state that blank preferred-term cells are not converted to zero counts.
findings.why_it_matters: Converting blank control cells to zero would create unsupported term-level comparative effects.
findings.recommended_action: Keep headache and other preferred-term rows descriptive unless explicit arm counts are later extracted.
